meta.migrationNotes: Existing torvikhauling.com is a weak Squarespace template. Rebuild from scratch; preserve domain, phone, email, and Google Business Profile. Set up 301 redirects from /services-1 → /services and /appointments → /contact.
unresolvedQuestions: FAQ content not explicitly provided by client — 6 FAQs drafted from common junk-removal objections; confirm at review.
Owner's name not confirmed — About page written in first person without naming. Testimonial mentions 'Matt'; delivered photos show a single operator (likely Jaron). Confirm before publish.
Schaumburg service area: existing site lists it, intake does not. Spec drops Schaumburg per intake list. Confirm.
Google review count: unknown. Display uses '5-star rated' without count; consider live Google Places API pull.
Before/after photo permission: no before/after photos in delivered pack. Flag if client sends any later.
Social URLs: delivered as share-links with tracking params; swap to canonical URLs before publish.
Additional job-site photos: delivered pack is brand/vehicle-heavy. Gallery would benefit from 3-5 in-progress job photos or before/after pairs — request mid-build.

contradictions.description: Existing site lists Schaumburg; intake does not.
contradictions.resolution: Drop Schaumburg. Confirm with client before publish.

contradictions.description: Intake: 'service area only, no address'. Existing site: 'Hawthorn Woods, IL'.
contradictions.resolution: Hawthorn Woods, IL in LocalBusiness schema for local SEO. No street address published publicly.

identity.description: Torvik Hauling is a local, family-owned junk removal and light demolition company serving the Northwest suburbs of Chicago. We handle everything from single-item pickups to full property cleanouts, always showing up on time and getting the job done right. Our goal is simple: leave every space cleaner than we found it while making the process easy for our neighbors.
identity.shortDescription: Family-owned junk removal & light demolition serving Lake Zurich and the Northwest Chicago suburbs. Licensed, insured, 1-2 hour response.
identity.primaryRegion: Lake County & Northwest Cook County, Illinois
identity.estimatesPolicy: Free estimates — quotes typically starting around $125

hoursOfOperation.note: Not confirmed by client; default to standard service hours. Verify before publish.

social._preLaunchTask: Replace share-link URLs with canonical profile URLs before publish.

colors._source: .design/palette.json (warm-trades aesthetic with cool-blue palette override per client brand direction)

brand.tone: Professional & trustworthy, bold & powerful, neighborly. Family-owned. No hype, no stereotypical junk-removal megaphone energy.
styleDirectives: Deep navy primary (#0B2E5B) — trust, headlines, primary CTAs
Cool light-blue accent (#6FA8DC) — ≤8% of any viewport (eco callouts, highlights)
Warm paper off-white background (#F7F5F0) — grounded, tactile feel
Chunky slab-serif display (Recoleta), clean sans body (Inter)
NO orange, yellow, or green (explicit client avoid list)
NO stereotypical junk-removal aesthetics (no orange trucks, no 'haul it all' megaphone energy, no before/after arrows)
NO stock people photos — use delivered Torvik photos or photorealistic equipment imagery
Soft 1px borders, 8% opacity shadows on elevated surfaces only

logo._preLaunchTask: Vectorize to SVG from .ai source for crisp scaling; use 2x PNG as interim.

ogImage.composite: 1200x630 — deep-navy bg, solo mark centered-left, 'Torvik Hauling — Junk Removal & Light Demolition' + 'Lake Zurich, IL' on the right in Recoleta white

photos._notes: All 5 delivered photos are portrait-captured but may render rotated. Respect EXIF orientation during build; crop/orient intentionally per usage.

hero.alt: Torvik Hauling truck and branded dump trailer parked on a Lake Zurich residential street under a bright cloudy sky
hero.usage: Primary hero image on home page
hero.reason: Wide shot of full truck+trailer combo on residential street with beautiful sky — shows scale, brand, and suburban-residential context. Strongest single hero candidate.

secondaryHero.alt: Side view of the Torvik Hauling dump trailer displaying the full services list and phone number (224) 456-6607
secondaryHero.usage: Services page hero, CTA band, footer accent
secondaryHero.reason: Trailer with full branding, phone, website, and services list in frame — doubles as a silent business card.

owner.alt: Torvik Hauling owner smiling in a branded black Torvik polo and cap beside the Torvik dump trailer
owner.reason: Warm, direct-to-camera owner portrait. Humanizes the 'family-owned' positioning. Caption ambiguity — see unresolvedQuestions about owner name.

action.alt: Torvik Hauling owner giving a thumbs-up next to a trailer loaded with boxes, bags, and household junk mid-haul
action.usage: Gallery lead card, How It Works 'Haul or Demo' step, services grid hover state
action.reason: Action shot mid-job — thumbs-up conveys 'done right', loaded trailer conveys capability.

eco.alt: Torvik Hauling dump trailer unloading construction and household debris at a licensed waste transfer station
eco.usage: Eco-friendly disposal section, How It Works 'Clean & Done' step, gallery
eco.reason: Direct visual proof of legitimate disposal — supports eco/recycle/donate messaging without greenwashing.

gallerySupplementNeeded.reason: Delivered pack is brand/vehicle-heavy. Gallery target is 6-10 images with variety. Missing: in-progress job photos at customer properties, before/after pairs, demolition action shots, cleanout interior shots.
gallerySupplementNeeded.requestToClient: Can you send 3-5 photos of actual jobs in progress — mid-cleanout rooms, deck/shed demo, before/after if any? These convert much harder than vehicle shots.

nav.mobileBehavior: hamburger menu, sticky phone button visible on all viewports <768px

columns.heading: Torvik Hauling
items: Serving Lake County & NW Cook County, IL

bottom.disclaimer: Licensed & Insured. Serving Lake Zurich, Hawthorn Woods, Barrington, and the Northwest Chicago suburbs.

primary.name: Junk Removal
primary.shortDescription: Fast, full-service junk removal for homes and businesses — single items to whole-truck loads. We do the lifting, sweep up after, and haul everything away responsibly.
primary.fullDescription: Whether it's one bulky item or a truckload of accumulated stuff, Torvik Hauling takes it off your hands quickly and cleanly. We handle residential and commercial junk: couches, mattresses, appliances, e-waste, furniture, yard debris, construction materials — if it's in the way, we'll get it out. We respond to inquiries within 1-2 hours, arrive on time, lift the heavy things so you don't have to, and leave your space spotless. Items are sorted for recycling or donation whenever possible; the rest goes to a licensed disposal facility.
features: Single-item pickup (sofas, mattresses, appliances, e-waste)
Whole-truck loads and multi-room clean-outs
Commercial and office junk removal
No hidden fees — free estimate before we start
We sweep up and leave the space cleaner than we found it
benefits: 1-2 hour response to calls, texts, and web requests
Licensed & insured — your property is protected
Eco-friendly: we recycle and donate whenever possible
Free on-site or phone estimates, typically starting around $125
Family-owned — you deal with the owner, not a call center

faqs.q: What items do you take?
faqs.a: Almost anything non-hazardous: furniture, mattresses, appliances, electronics, yard debris, construction waste, boxes, bagged trash. We can't take liquid paint, chemicals, propane tanks, or asbestos — but we can usually point you to who can.

faqs.q: Do I need to prep the junk before you arrive?
faqs.a: No — leave it where it is. We'll do the lifting, loading, and cleanup. If you've already bagged or stacked things, great; if not, we'll handle it.

faqs.q: How is pricing determined?
faqs.a: Volume-based, not hourly. We give you a firm quote after a quick look (on-site or via photos) before we load anything. Most jobs start around $125 for a single item or small load.

faqs.q: How fast can you come out?
faqs.a: We respond to inquiries in 1-2 hours and can often schedule same-day or next-day pickup for the Lake Zurich / NW Cook County area.

hero.headline: Junk Removal in Lake Zurich & Northwest Chicago
hero.subheadline: One-item pickups to full-truck loads. 1-2 hour response, free estimates starting around $125.

primary.name: Property Cleanouts
primary.shortDescription: Full-home, estate, foreclosure, hoarder, and rental turnover cleanouts handled discreetly and thoroughly — empty to broom-swept, start to finish.
primary.fullDescription: Cleanouts are heavy emotional work, and we treat them that way. Torvik Hauling handles full-property cleanouts for homeowners, estate executors, property managers, and realtors across the Northwest Chicago suburbs. We take the entire contents: furniture, appliances, clothing, boxes, yard items, anything left behind — and leave the property broom-swept and ready for the next step, whether that's listing, renting, or moving in. We work quickly and privately; neighbors see a clean truck, not chaos.
features: Estate and probate cleanouts — coordinated with executors and realtors
Foreclosure and bank-owned property cleanouts
Hoarder and difficult-cleanout situations handled with discretion
Move-in / move-out and rental turnover cleanouts
Broom-swept finish — photo-ready for listings
benefits: One point of contact — the owner — from quote to final sweep
Licensed & insured, so property managers and executors are protected
Fast scheduling — often same-week for full cleanouts
Donation and recycling routing for usable items
Written quote before we start; no surprise add-ons

faqs.q: How quickly can you finish a full-home cleanout?
faqs.a: Most 2-3 bedroom cleanouts finish in one day. Larger or more cluttered homes run 1-3 days. We'll give you a specific timeline in the quote.

faqs.q: Can you handle a hoarder-type situation?
faqs.a: Yes — we've taken on jobs others declined. We'll walk through with you first, discuss what's worth saving vs. disposing, and handle the job without judgment.

faqs.q: Do you coordinate with realtors and estate attorneys?
faqs.a: Absolutely. We regularly work with realtors preparing listings and executors settling estates. We can schedule around closing dates and provide documentation.

faqs.q: What happens to usable items?
faqs.a: Clothing, furniture in good condition, and household goods are routed to local donation centers whenever possible. If you want items kept for family, mark them before we arrive.

hero.headline: Full Property Cleanouts — Estate, Foreclosure, Rental Turnover
hero.subheadline: Discreet, thorough, broom-swept finish. One call from quote to done.

primary.name: Light Demolition
primary.shortDescription: Shed, deck, fence, playset, hot tub, and above-ground pool removal — plus interior non-structural demo. We tear it down, haul it out, clean it up.
primary.fullDescription: Torvik Hauling handles the light-demolition jobs that full-service contractors don't want and DIY homeowners shouldn't attempt. We safely dismantle and remove decks, sheds, fences, playsets, hot tubs, and above-ground pools — plus interior non-structural demo like cabinets, vanities, flooring, and drywall. Every job includes debris haul-away and cleanup, so you're left with a clear footprint ready for whatever's next. We're equipped for heavy loads (concrete, scrap metal) and unusual items most haulers won't touch.
features: Deck, fence, and pergola removal
Shed and outbuilding demolition + haul-away
Hot tub and above-ground pool removal
Playset and swing-set dismantling
Interior demo: cabinets, vanities, flooring, drywall (non-structural)
benefits: One crew for demo + haul-away — no coordinating dumpster rental
Equipped for heavy items (hot tubs, concrete, steel) others decline
Insured — your property and neighbors are protected during demo
Property left level and swept, ready for re-build or re-landscape
Free on-site estimate — we walk the job with you first

faqs.q: Do I need a permit for demo work?
faqs.a: Most residential light demo (decks, sheds, hot tubs) doesn't require a permit in the Lake County / NW Cook County suburbs, but rules vary by municipality. We'll flag anything we think needs one before starting.

faqs.q: Can you handle concrete and heavy materials?
faqs.a: Yes. We have the equipment and hauling capacity for concrete, brick, and scrap metal. Heavy loads are priced separately from standard debris because of disposal costs.

faqs.q: Will you break up a hot tub before hauling?
faqs.a: Usually yes. We dismantle hot tubs on-site and haul the pieces — faster and easier than trying to move a full tub through tight yards or gates.

faqs.q: Do you do interior demo during a renovation?
faqs.a: Yes — kitchen cabinets, bathroom vanities, flooring, and non-structural drywall. We work alongside GCs or directly with homeowners. Structural work (load-bearing walls, roofing) is outside our scope.

hero.headline: Light Demolition & Heavy-Item Removal
hero.subheadline: Decks, sheds, hot tubs, pools, interior demo. We tear down, haul out, clean up — all under one crew.

primary.name: Garage, Basement & Attic Cleanouts
primary.shortDescription: Top-to-bottom clearouts for garages, basements, attics, and storage units. We sort, haul, and sweep so you get your space back.
primary.fullDescription: Years of accumulation don't clear themselves. Torvik Hauling turns a full garage or basement into an empty, broom-swept space — usually in a single visit. We handle the boxes nobody opened, the furniture that's been 'temporary' for a decade, the broken appliances, and the holiday decorations that never made it to the curb. Sort-as-we-go: we can pull out anything you want to keep or donate, and the rest goes to proper disposal. Storage-unit cleanouts also welcome.
features: Full-garage clearouts (one-car to three-car)
Basement cleanouts — including items movers won't touch
Attic cleanouts, insulation and all
Storage-unit emptying (we meet you at the facility)
Sorting assistance — keep, donate, dispose piles
benefits: Single-day turnaround for most residential cleanouts
We bring the dollies, straps, and manpower — you don't lift a thing
Donation routing for usable items reduces waste and landfill cost
Post-cleanout sweep so the space is photo-ready or use-ready
Free estimate, firm quote — no drive-time or 'while we're here' add-ons

faqs.q: Can you cleanout a storage unit I can't access easily?
faqs.a: Yes. We meet you at the storage facility, load the contents, and handle disposal. Bring your key or access code; we bring everything else.

faqs.q: What if I want to keep some items?
faqs.a: Tell us what's staying before we load. We sort as we go and set aside anything you want to keep or donate.

faqs.q: Do you clean the space after?
faqs.a: Every cleanout includes a sweep-out. Most garages and basements look empty and clean when we leave — not empty and grimy.

faqs.q: Can you handle heavy items like old treadmills or piano keyboards?
faqs.a: Yes. Heavy and awkward items are what we do. Bring them on.

hero.headline: Garage, Basement & Attic Cleanouts
hero.subheadline: Get the space back. One-day turnaround, swept clean, free estimate.

primary.name: Outdoor & Yard Cleanup
primary.shortDescription: Yard waste, storm debris, brush, branches, dirt, rock, and landscaping haul-away. We clear the yard so you can use it again.
primary.fullDescription: After a big storm, a landscaping project, or just a season of neglect, yards accumulate. Torvik Hauling clears yard waste, fallen branches, brush piles, storm debris, dirt, rock, and landscaping materials — including the loads most garbage pickups refuse. We show up with the right equipment, load fast, and haul to proper disposal or compost facilities. No dragging bags to the curb for weeks of pickups, no calling the municipality to negotiate large-debris rules.
features: Storm debris and tree-limb cleanup (post-storm emergency available)
Brush and branch removal from back yards and woodlines
Dirt, rock, concrete, and paver haul-away
Landscaping debris after a renovation or re-grade
Sod removal and disposal
benefits: Clears what regular garbage pickup won't — no bag limits, no size limits
Composting and recycling for organic yard waste when possible
Fast turnaround after storms (1-2 hour response)
No repeat trips — one visit, yard cleared
Insured for working on and around landscaping you want protected

faqs.q: Can you come right after a storm?
faqs.a: Yes. Post-storm cleanup is one of our faster-response services — we can usually be out within 24 hours after major weather events.

faqs.q: Do you take concrete and rock?
faqs.a: Yes. Heavy materials (concrete, rock, brick, pavers) are priced by weight because disposal costs more than standard yard waste. We'll give you a firm number first.

faqs.q: What about yard waste that's too big for the garbage company?
faqs.a: That's exactly what we're for. Branches over a few inches, large shrub root-balls, stumps under a certain size — we handle it.

faqs.q: Can you remove a fallen tree?
faqs.a: If it's already down and sectioned, yes. If it needs to be felled and cut, we'd partner you with a tree service for the cutting and then handle the haul-away.

hero.headline: Yard & Outdoor Cleanup
hero.subheadline: Storm debris, brush, dirt, rock, landscaping waste. We haul what your garbage company won't.

primary.name: Construction & Renovation Debris
primary.shortDescription: Job-site debris hauling for contractors, remodelers, and DIY homeowners. Concrete, scrap metal, drywall, flooring — gone.
primary.fullDescription: Renovations generate more waste than most people expect — old cabinets, ripped-up flooring, drywall, concrete, scrap metal, and packaging. Torvik Hauling handles construction and renovation debris for GCs, remodelers, and homeowners doing DIY projects. Cheaper and more flexible than a roll-off dumpster for small-to-mid jobs: you don't pay for the days it sits in your driveway, and we can come back for repeat loads on a phased project. We're set up for heavy materials (concrete, scrap metal) that weight-based disposal makes expensive.
features: Residential renovation cleanout (kitchen, bath, flooring tear-outs)
Contractor job-site debris pickup — repeat loads as the job progresses
Concrete removal and disposal
Scrap metal haul-away (we route to recyclers)
Drywall, tile, and flooring haul-off
benefits: Flexibility — no dumpster rental, no 'ran out of space' stress
Repeat-load pricing for multi-phase jobs
Scrap metal gets recycled, not landfilled
Insured for working around active job sites
Fast response so your crew isn't waiting on a dumpster swap

faqs.q: Is this cheaper than a dumpster rental?
faqs.a: For small and mid-sized jobs, usually yes — you only pay for what we haul, not rental days. For very large jobs with continuous output, a dumpster is more efficient; we'll tell you honestly which way makes sense.

faqs.q: Can you come multiple times during a project?
faqs.a: Yes. We do phased haul-away for ongoing renos — call when a load is ready, we come get it.

faqs.q: Do you work with contractors on job sites?
faqs.a: Regularly. We can invoice the GC directly, coordinate with site supers, and work around active crews.

faqs.q: What about heavy materials like concrete?
faqs.a: Yes — we're equipped for concrete, masonry, and scrap metal. These are weight-priced separately because of disposal costs.

hero.headline: Construction & Renovation Debris Haul-Away
hero.subheadline: For GCs, remodelers, and DIY homeowners. Cheaper than a dumpster for most jobs.

testimonials.quote: They helped me move two sofas and were incredibly professional, timely, and efficient from start to finish. I really appreciated how careful and considerate they were with the space. Matt was great to work with — very responsive and accommodating. I needed the sofas removed within 24 hours, and he made it happen seamlessly.
testimonials.author: Hajira Mohammed

testimonials.quote: Super fast and professional. They took a rather challenging job that I have had others deny in the past. They were well equipped with tools to demo and remove an existing steel furnace drum that was original to a hundred year old house. Glad to finally have my space back in my basement. Torvik will be my go-to contractor in my personal and professional projects.
testimonials.author: Matt M.

testimonials.quote: Prompt, speedy, affordable and a pleasure to work with. Showed up and removed our hot tub and made sure the area was all cleaned up when he was done.
testimonials.author: Isabelle

items.label: 5-Star Rated
items.note: Google reviews — count TBD or pulled live

processSteps.title: Contact
processSteps.description: Call, text, or fill out the form for a fast, free estimate. We respond in 1-2 hours — not the next business day.

processSteps.title: Quote & Schedule
processSteps.description: We confirm pricing up front — typically starting around $125 — and book a pickup that fits your schedule. No surprise fees after we arrive.

processSteps.title: Haul or Demo
processSteps.description: We show up on time, do the heavy lifting, and handle the demo if needed. You don't move a thing unless you want to.

processSteps.title: Clean & Done
processSteps.description: We leave your space cleaner than we found it — broom-swept, no trace. You pay only when you're satisfied.

faqs.q: What items do you take?
faqs.a: Almost anything non-hazardous: furniture, mattresses, appliances, electronics, yard debris, construction waste, hot tubs, decks, sheds, and full cleanouts. We don't take liquid paint, chemicals, propane tanks, or asbestos — but we can usually point you to who can.

faqs.q: How much does it cost?
faqs.a: Volume-based, not hourly. Every job gets a free, firm estimate before we load anything — most jobs start around $125 for a single item or small load. Larger jobs and heavy materials (concrete, scrap metal) are priced by the load or by weight. No surprises after we arrive.

faqs.q: How fast can you come out?
faqs.a: We respond to calls, texts, and form submissions within 1-2 hours — not the next business day. Same-day or next-day pickup is usually available for the Lake Zurich and Northwest Cook County area.

faqs.q: Are you licensed and insured?
faqs.a: Yes — fully licensed and insured. Your property, and ours, is protected on every job. Property managers, executors, and realtors can request insurance documentation before we start.

faqs.q: Do you clean up after the job?
faqs.a: Every job ends with a sweep-out. Garages, basements, and yards look genuinely clean when we leave — not just 'empty.' For property cleanouts, we broom-sweep so the space is photo-ready.

faqs.q: What happens to the items you haul away?
faqs.a: We recycle and donate whenever possible. Usable furniture, clothing, and household goods are routed to local donation centers; scrap metal and e-waste go to recyclers; the rest goes to a licensed disposal facility. Less landfill is good for everyone.

seo.metaTitle: About Torvik Hauling — Family-Owned Junk Removal in Lake Zurich
seo.metaDescription: Torvik Hauling is a family-owned junk removal & demolition company serving NW Chicago suburbs. Our story, our approach, and why we do this work.

sections.headline: Family Owned. Community Focused.
sections.subheadline: We're not a franchise. We're a local family-owned hauling company working one neighborhood at a time.

sections.headline: Why we do this work
sections.body:
Torvik Hauling was built on a simple idea: show up, work hard, and do the job right.

After years in corporate America, I knew I wanted to build something of my own — not just for myself, but to show my daughter that anything is possible with effort and consistency. What started as helping friends and neighbors quickly turned into something more.

There's nothing better than seeing the look on someone's face after a space is cleared out — that sense of relief and a fresh start. That's what drives me every day. We're not just hauling junk, we're helping people take back their space and feel good about it.
sections._note: First-person until owner name is confirmed.

items.title: Show up on time.
items.body: When we say 1-2 hour response, we mean it. Not 'next business day.'

items.title: Do the job right.
items.body: Swept clean, not just empty. If it's not the way we'd want it at our own house, it's not done.

items.title: Handle the hard stuff.
items.body: Hot tubs, steel drums from 100-year-old basements, hoarder situations. We take on what others decline.

items.title: Send less to the landfill.
items.body: Donation and recycling first. Disposal last.

schema.description: Family-owned junk removal and light demolition company serving the Northwest Chicago suburbs.

aggregateRating._note: Update reviewCount when live Google count is known or pulled via Places API

aiDiscoverabilityNotes: Add FAQ structured data (schema.org/FAQPage) to homepage and all service-detail pages.
Add Service schema (schema.org/Service) on each service-detail page linked to the LocalBusiness.
Use HowTo schema (schema.org/HowTo) on the 'How It Works' section for rich-result eligibility.
Every H1 includes the primary city + service combination (e.g., 'Junk Removal in Lake Zurich & Northwest Chicago').
Keep NAP (name, address, phone) consistent: 'Torvik Hauling' · 'Hawthorn Woods, IL' · '(224) 456-6607' on every page (footer is canonical).
Individual service-detail pages + service-areas page together give Torvik city-specific landing coverage — the biggest gap on the existing Squarespace site.
Add robots.txt + sitemap.xml at build time; submit to Google Search Console after launch.
Verify Google Business Profile matches the new site's NAP exactly after launch.

analytics._note: Client uses Google Ads. Recommend implementing these before publish.

googleTagManager.containerId: TBD — create and provide to client

googleAnalytics4.measurementId: TBD — create and provide to client

googleAdsConversion.notes: Set up conversion tracking on form submits and phone-click events to measure paid-ad ROI.

metaPixel.notes: Add only if client starts Meta ads.

requirements: All images have descriptive alt text (see $.photos for written alts)
Color contrast ≥ 4.5:1 for body text, ≥ 3:1 for large text
Forms have labels associated with inputs, visible focus states
Keyboard navigation works for all interactive elements including gallery lightbox and mobile nav
Phone numbers in tel: links; email in mailto: links
Map has text-list fallback ($.identity.serviceAreas.full) for screen readers
Honor prefers-reduced-motion — disable scroll parallax and reveal animations

recommended.styling: Tailwind CSS with design tokens from $.brand
recommended.cms: none (content lives in SPEC.json + MDX if needed)
recommended.formHandling: Vercel serverless function → email via Resend + honeypot
recommended.analytics: GTM + GA4 + Google Ads conversion tracking

domain.migration: Point DNS to Vercel once new site is production-ready. Keep domain registration; migrate DNS only.

launchChecklist: Replace Instagram/Facebook share-links with canonical profile URLs
Confirm FAQ copy with client (6 drafted)
Confirm Schaumburg dropped from service areas
Confirm hours of operation (default values used)
Vectorize logo .ai → SVG for crisp scaling
Obtain Google review count OR wire live Places API call
Request 3-5 more in-progress job photos to strengthen gallery
Verify Google Business Profile NAP matches site
Set up GTM + GA4 + Google Ads conversion tracking IDs
Submit sitemap.xml to Google Search Console
Run Lighthouse + Axe accessibility audit pre-publish
If Jobber active by launch: add Jobber lead-intake webhook as secondary form destination
